entries.prompt_text: Change the {market size} in the {executive summary} to {$6.5B}
Change the {company headcount} in {paragraph 2} to {147}
Update the {launch year} in the {company overview} to {2019}
Change the {Q4 revenue} in the {sidebar callout} to {$3.1M}
Update the {case count} in {Table 2} to {84}
Change the {survey year} in {footnote 3} to {2024}
Update the {office location} in the {footer} to {Chicago, IL}
Change the {adoption rate} in the {chart caption} to {27%}
Update the {CEO name} in the {leadership section} to {J. Martinez}
Change the {founding year} in the {about us} paragraph to {2017}
Update the {customer count} in the {key metrics} callout to {2,400}
Change the {patent number} in the {product description} to {US 11,234,567}
Update the {industry ranking} in the {introduction} from {#5} to {#3}
Change the {contract value} in the {deal summary} to {$1.8M}
Update the {compliance standard} in the {requirements section} from {ISO 9001} to {ISO 27001}
Add a source line under {Figure 2}
Move the source note under the {chart} instead of inside the {caption}
Replace the placeholder citation in {footnote 4} with a real source
Add the {publication year} to the source note under {Table 1}
Change the source line under the {bar chart} to {Source: Company filings, 2024}
Replace {internal research} in the {footnote} with the actual report title
Remove the broken web link from the citation in the {references section}
Add the missing source note under the {market share table}
Add a source attribution under {Figure 1} that currently has no citation
Change the vague source {various reports} under the {pie chart} to a specific report name
Add the {author name} to the source note under {Table 3} that only lists a year
Replace {TBD} in the {footnote} with the actual source reference
Add a source line under the {infographic} that currently has no attribution
Update the source note under the {revenue chart} to include the {retrieval date}
Remove the self-referential source note that says {Source: this document}
Spell out {EBITDA} on first use in the {findings} section
Spell out {TAM} on first use in the {market overview}
Change {ARR} in the {callout} to {annual recurring revenue}
Change {MRR} in the {table note} to {monthly recurring revenue}
Replace {net retention} in the {sidebar} with {net revenue retention}
Replace {SLA} in the {terms section} with {service level agreement}
Change {legal team} in the {disclaimer} to {counsel}
Change {uptime} in the {KPI box} to {service availability}
Spell out {CAGR} on first use in the {growth summary}
Replace {NPS} in the {customer section} with {Net Promoter Score}
Spell out {ROI} on first use in the {recommendation paragraph}
Change {FTE} in the {staffing table} to {full-time equivalent}
Replace {CAC} in the {metrics callout} with {customer acquisition cost}
Spell out {LTV} on first use in the {unit economics} section
Replace {SOW} in the {project overview} with {statement of work}
Replace {manpower} with {staffing} in the {implementation plan}
Replace {chairman} with {chair} in the {governance note}
Replace {he or she} with {they} in the {hiring section}
Replace {master list} with {main list} in the {data section}
Replace {disabled employees} with {employees with disabilities} in the {policy note}
Replace {native English speaker} with {fluent English speaker} in the {job requirements}
Replace {elderly customers} with {older customers} in the {market profile}
Remove the word {guys} from the {team note}
Replace {mankind} with {humanity} in the {mission statement}
Replace {whitelist} with {allowlist} in the {technical requirements}
Replace {blacklist} with {blocklist} in the {security section}
Replace {manmade} with {manufactured} in the {product description}
Replace {the blind} with {people who are blind} in the {accessibility section}
Replace {grandfathered} with {legacy} in the {pricing note}
Replace {manning the booth} with {staffing the booth} in the {event plan}
Remove the {Lorem ipsum} placeholder text in the {introduction} paragraph
Replace {[INSERT NAME HERE]} in the {title block} with the actual name
Remove the {TODO: add data} note left in the {findings} section
Replace {XX%} in the {summary callout} with the actual percentage
Remove the {DRAFT} watermark text that was left in the {header}
Replace {[Company Name]} in the {footer} with the actual company name
Remove the {TBD} placeholder in the {timeline} row for {Phase 3}
Replace {$X.XM} in the {budget table} with the actual dollar amount
Remove the {[Source needed]} note left in {footnote 2}
Replace {YYYY-MM-DD} in the {revision date} field with the actual date
Remove the {sample text} paragraph left over from the template
Replace {[Author]} in the {byline} with the actual author name
Remove the {DELETE THIS SECTION} comment left in the {notes} area
Replace {N/A} in the {results table} with the actual value or remove the row
Remove the {[Placeholder image]} label where the chart should be
Define {KPI} on first use in the {metrics section}
Add the full form of {SaaS} the first time it appears in the {overview}
Spell out {API} on first use in the {technical requirements} section
Define {ERP} on first use in the {systems paragraph}
Add the full form of {CRM} the first time it appears in the {tools section}
Spell out {OKR} on first use in the {goals section}
Define {B2B} on first use in the {market analysis}
Add the full form of {P&L} the first time it appears in the {financials section}
Spell out {QA} on first use in the {process section}
Define {MVP} on first use in the {product roadmap}
Add the full form of {UAT} the first time it appears in the {testing section}
Spell out {POC} on first use in the {proposal section}
Define {RFP} on first use in the {procurement section}
Add the full form of {ETL} the first time it appears in the {data pipeline section}
Spell out {SSO} on first use in the {security requirements}
Change {03/15/2025} in the {header} to {March 15, 2025} to match the rest of the document
Change {2025-03-15} in the {footer} to {March 15, 2025} for consistency
Change {15 March 2025} in the {timeline table} to {March 15, 2025} to match other dates
Change {Mar 15} in the {callout box} to {March 15, 2025} to include the full year
Change {Q1 25} in the {chart label} to {Q1 2025} for clarity
Change {3/15} in the {table cell} to {March 15, 2025} to match the document format
Standardize all dates in {Table 1} to use {Month DD, YYYY} format
Change {FY25} in the {budget heading} to {FY2025} for consistency
Add the year to the date in the {revision line} that only shows {March 15}
Change {15/03/2025} in the {contract section} to {March 15, 2025} to match US format
Change the abbreviated month in {Jan 2025} in the {sidebar} to {January 2025}
Standardize the mixed date formats in the {timeline section} to a single format
Change {1st Quarter 2025} in the {summary} to {Q1 2025} to match other quarterly labels
Add the day to the date in the {footnote} that only shows {March 2025}
Change {2025/03/15} in the {metadata block} to {March 15, 2025} for consistency
Change {Acme Corp} in {paragraph 3} to {Acme Corporation} to match the rest of the document
Change {J. Smith} in the {footer} to {Jane Smith} to match the {header}
Change {Project Alpha} in the {timeline} to {Project Apex} to match the {title}
Standardize the product name to {DataSync Pro} throughout the document instead of mixing {DataSync} and {Data Sync}
Change {the client} in {paragraph 5} to {Globex Industries} to match the named reference elsewhere
Change {Dr. Chen} in the {acknowledgments} to {Dr. R. Chen} to match the {references}
Standardize the department name to {Engineering} instead of alternating between {Engineering} and {Product Engineering}
Change {VP of Sales} in the {org chart} to {Vice President of Sales} to match the {team section}
Change {US} in the {market section} to {U.S.} to match the rest of the document
Standardize the vendor name to {AWS} instead of alternating between {AWS} and {Amazon Web Services} after first use
Change {Q4 FY24} in the {table header} to {Q4 FY2024} to match other quarterly labels
Change {the Board} in the {governance note} to {the Board of Directors} to match the {introduction}
Standardize the team name to {Customer Success} instead of mixing {CS} and {Customer Success} after first mention
Change {NYC} in the {location section} to {New York} to match other city references
Change {Phase 1} in the {summary} to {Phase I} to match the {timeline section} numbering
